title: Codex Task

result.rawOutput:
**Findings**
1. `Medium` In [service.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/core/service.py), the MR branch’s stale guard runs before DB dedupe/persistence. That prevents duplicate replies, but it also means a replayed stale event is never recorded. If the upstream keeps backfilling the same old message on every reconnect, this code will re-parse, re-check, and re-log it every time across pods/restarts instead of suppressing it after first sighting. That is the main performance/operational gap in the current design.

2. `Low` In [service.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/core/service.py), in-flight dedupe happens before stale classification. Two concurrent deliveries of the same stale message can therefore split into different outcomes: one request returns `accepted=false, reason=stale_message`, while the other can return `accepted=true, deduplicated=true`. No duplicate side effect occurs, but the result is timing-dependent and will skew caller behavior/metrics.

3. `Low` In [feishu.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/feishu.py), [discord.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/discord.py), and [telegram.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/transport/telegram.py), unparseable or missing upstream timestamps fail open to `create_time=None`, which makes the stale filter silently skip the message. That is backward-compatible, but there is no warning/metric when the protection is disabled this way, so an upstream payload change would quietly reintroduce stale-message processing.

4. `Low` In [service.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/core/service.py), the stale-drop warning attaches `stale_age_ms` and `threshold_ms`, but [logging_config.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/logging_config.py) only serializes a small allowlist of extra fields. In JSON logs, the new diagnostic values are dropped, so operators will not actually see the age/threshold numbers needed to tune or debug false positives.

**Coverage gaps**
- [tests/test_channel_service.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/tests/test_channel_service.py) covers stale, fresh, missing `create_time`, zero threshold, and default threshold, but it misses exact-boundary behavior (`age == threshold`), negative thresholds, future timestamps / clock skew, repeated stale replays, and concurrent duplicate stale deliveries.
- There is no config-wiring coverage for [settings.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/config/settings.py), [service_config.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/config/service_config.py), and [app.py](/Users/liuchunlei/Code/skywork/agent/channels/src/channels/api/app.py) to prove TOML/env configuration reaches `ChannelService`.
- There is no explicit assertion that stale drops short-circuit downstream side effects such as DB `save()` or chat WS sends.

**Notes**
Reviewed `master...feat/stale-message-filter` (merge-base is current `master`, commit `48b9e71`; MR tip is `039b10f`). I did not run the test suite because the sandbox is read-only and blocks normal temp-file/test execution.
